intro: Lastrea rosenstockii, also known as the New Zealand fern, is a species of fern belonging to the genus Lastrea, characterized by its delicate, lacy fronds and ability to thrive in a variety of environments. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which are thought to aid in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.
